openmeter/billing/rating.purpose: Public contract layer for the billing rating sub-system: defines the Service interface, DetailedLine value type, accessor interfaces (StandardLineAccessor, GatheringLineAccessor), and ChildUniqueReferenceID constants consumed by the rating/service implementation, charges, and billing worker. Callers import only this package — never rating/service.

patterns.name: ChildUniqueReferenceID constants from const.go
patterns.description: All child line identifiers must use the named constants in const.go (e.g. UsageChildUniqueReferenceID, UnitPriceUsageChildUniqueReferenceID). Never hardcode these strings in pricers, mutators, or callers.

patterns.name: currencyx.Calculator for all monetary arithmetic
patterns.description: Every amount on DetailedLine must be rounded via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision. Raw alpacadecimal arithmetic is only acceptable as an intermediate step before storage.

patterns.name: Functional options for GenerateDetailedLines variants
patterns.description: Behavioural knobs (IgnoreMinimumCommitment, DisableCreditsMutator) are passed as variadic GenerateDetailedLinesOption functions. Never add boolean flags directly to the Service interface signature.

patterns.name: Accessor interfaces — never concrete billing line types
patterns.description: Callers supply lines via StandardLineAccessor or GatheringLineAccessor. The rating package must not import or reference concrete billing.InvoiceLine structs directly.

patterns.name: AddDiscountForOverage for max-spend capping
patterns.description: Maximum-spend cap discounts must be applied by calling DetailedLine.AddDiscountForOverage with an AddDiscountInput; returns a new value. Never compute overage discounts inline in pricers or service methods.
patterns.example: line = line.AddDiscountForOverage(rating.AddDiscountInput{BilledAmountBeforeLine: prev, MaxSpend: max, Currency: calc})

patterns.name: DetailedLine is an immutable value type
patterns.description: All With-style mutations (AddDiscountForOverage) return a new DetailedLine copy. Pricers must produce new values rather than mutating shared state. No pointer receivers that mutate in place.

key_file_guides.role: Single source of truth for all ChildUniqueReferenceID string values used by pricers, mutators, tests, and callers to label DetailedLine children.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new price type without a corresponding constant here causes silent mismatches in child-line correlation across charges and billing worker.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the DetailedLine value type, TotalAmount computation (PerUnitAmount×Quantity minus discounts and credits), AddDiscountForOverage, and DetailedLines.Sum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TotalAmount already subtracts AmountDiscounts and CreditsApplied — double-subtracting in callers produces incorrect invoice totals. Negative Quantity is valid (usage correction); negative PerUnitAmount is rejected by Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Accessor interfaces (StandardLineAccessor, GatheringLineAccessor, PriceAccessor) that decouple the rating package from concrete billing line types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding methods to these interfaces forces all implementors to update; prefer adding helpers in service.go or rating/service instead.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface, GenerateDetailedLinesResult, ResolveBillablePeriodInput (with Validate()), and functional option types. Concrete implementation lives in rating/service sub-package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ResolveBillablePeriodInput.Validate() is the authoritative check — do not duplicate this validation in callers or the implementation.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding ChildUniqueReferenceID strings instead of using the constants in const.go
Setting input.Usage for FlatPriceType lines — pricer validation returns an error
Computing DetailedLine totals (rounding, discount subtraction) outside TotalAmount or getTotalsFromDetailedLines in rating/service
Importing concrete billing line types (billing.InvoiceLine) directly in this package — use the accessor interfaces in line.go
Adding state fields to the service struct in rating/service — the implementation is intentionally stateless; New() returns an empty struct

decisions.decision: Service interface lives in the rating package root; implementation in rating/service sub-package
decisions.rationale: Callers (charges, billing worker) import only the interface and types without pulling in implementation dependencies such as pricer logic and mutator chains.

decisions.decision: DetailedLine is a value type with copy-on-write semantics for all mutations
decisions.rationale: Immutable-by-convention pipeline: pricers produce new DetailedLine values rather than mutating shared state, making mutation order explicit and preventing aliasing bugs in the priceMutator chain.

decisions.decision: Behavioural knobs use functional options (GenerateDetailedLinesOption) rather than input struct fields
decisions.rationale: Keeps the primary input path (StandardLineAccessor) stable while allowing opt-in overrides (IgnoreMinimumCommitment, DisableCreditsMutator) without breaking existing callers.

code_examples.scenario: Computing a DetailedLine total with max-spend overage discount
